evals.name: cursor-headless-implementation
evals.prompt: Use Cursor CLI to implement the approved API pagination changes in this repo. Keep it headless and summarize the changed files.
evals.expected_output: The skill should route the task to Cursor CLI, prefer `agent --print --trust`, and avoid adding stronger execution flags.
expectations: The response uses or recommends `agent --print --trust` for the headless implementation run.
The response makes clear that `agent` means the Cursor CLI command.
The response runs from the intended repository or workspace.
The response does not silently add `--force`, `--yolo`, `--sandbox disabled`, or `--approve-mcps`.

evals.name: cursor-review-ask-mode
evals.prompt: Use Cursor CLI to review the current git diff for bugs, compatibility issues, and hidden blast radius. Do not edit files.
evals.expected_output: The skill should use a headless Cursor CLI review prompt in ask mode that is review-only and findings-first.
expectations: The response uses or recommends `agent --print --trust --mode ask` for the review run.
The review prompt treats the diff as primary scope.
The review prompt requires bounded impact tracing through callers, references, consumers, contracts, compatibility assumptions, and immediate upstream/downstream paths.
The review prompt says not to edit files or start build/test work.

evals.name: cursor-plan-mode
evals.prompt: Use Cursor CLI in plan mode to compare two approaches for splitting the monolith service. I only want a plan, no file edits.
evals.expected_output: The skill should use Cursor CLI plan mode for read-only planning.
expectations: The response uses or recommends `agent --print --trust --mode plan`.
The response encodes the no-edit constraint.
The response does not use implementation mode for a planning-only task.
The response does not add stronger execution flags.

evals.name: cursor-model-pass-through
evals.prompt: Run Cursor CLI with model gpt-5.4-medium to investigate this regression and patch it if needed.
evals.expected_output: The skill should pass through the requested Cursor model without inventing unrelated defaults.
expectations: The response includes `--model gpt-5.4-medium`.
The response uses `agent --print --trust` if the run is headless or automated.
The response does not invent another model.
The response does not add unrelated sandbox or force flags.

evals.name: generic-subagent-no-cursor
evals.prompt: Please use a subagent to inspect the auth tests and report back. I do not care which built-in helper you use.
evals.expected_output: The skill should not trigger because the user asked for a generic or built-in subagent, not Cursor CLI.
expectations: The response does not introduce Cursor CLI `agent` commands.
The response treats the request as generic delegation rather than Cursor CLI selection.
The response may ask which external executor to use if external execution is required.

evals.name: generic-agent-word-no-cursor
evals.prompt: Have an agent review the changes and tell me if there are regressions. Any normal sub agent is fine.
evals.expected_output: The skill should not trigger merely because the user used the word agent.
expectations: The response does not interpret generic `agent` as the Cursor CLI command.
The response does not use `agent --print --trust`.
The response treats the request as generic review delegation or asks for executor choice.

evals.name: wrong-executor-codex
evals.prompt: Run Codex on this repo to implement the UI copy update. Keep it non-interactive.
evals.expected_output: The Cursor skill should not activate because the user selected Codex CLI, not Cursor CLI.
expectations: The response does not use Cursor CLI `agent` commands.
The response recognizes that Codex CLI is the selected executor.
The response does not substitute Cursor CLI for the named executor.

evals.name: cursor-editor-info-no-cli
evals.prompt: What is the difference between Cursor editor and VS Code for everyday coding? Do not run anything.
evals.expected_output: The skill should not launch Cursor CLI for a general explanation about Cursor editor.
expectations: The response does not launch or recommend `agent --print --trust`.
The response respects the instruction not to run anything.
The response treats the task as normal explanation.
